steps.title: Use the midpoint condition to find key distances
steps.content: P is the midpoint of $\overline{NQ}$, so $NP = PQ = 52$ feet. Therefore, $NQ = 52 + 52 = 104$ feet.

steps.title: Calculate the semicircle and rectangle areas
steps.content:
Since $\overline{NP}$ is the diameter of the semicircle, the radius is $r = \frac{52}{2} = 26$ feet.

Semicircle area: $$A_{\text{semicircle}} = \frac{1}{2}\pi r^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2 = \frac{1}{2}\pi(676) = 338\pi$$
The rectangle $NQRS$ has width $NQ = 104$ feet and height $NS = 18$ feet:
$$A_{\text{rectangle}} = 104 \times 18 = 1872 \text{ square feet}$$

steps.title: Find a - b from the total area
steps.content:
Total area of the figure: $$A_{\text{total}} = 338\pi + 1872$$
Comparing to $a\pi + b$, we have $a = 338$ and $b = 1872$. Therefore, $a - b = 338 - 1872 = -1534$.
